[2020-12-15 17:27] velizar_ (Name): The most commonly played game mode of Starcraft 2 is 1v1, where you start with just a base and a few workers;
[2020-12-15 17:28] velizar_ (Name): Starcraft 2 has campaign, which has a variety of things - sometimes you don't have a base and only need to micro-manage your army; sometimes you start with a base, and generally have a different army from 1vs1; you can even customize your units with spendable currency
[2020-12-15 17:28] velizar_ (Name): and you can also get global-use abilities which are also customizable
[2020-12-15 17:29] velizar_ (Name): in that you don't usually need to kill your opponent, instead you have some objective and sometimes you're on a timer
[2020-12-15 17:29] velizar_ (Name): Separately, there's co-op mode where you and another human play, and you each have about 15 different "races" to choose from, and play a variety of possible scenarios - those "races" have very different play styles, and a variety of strategies, and they synergize differently with each other, also the enemy unit composition varies from game to game;
[2020-12-15 17:30] velizar_ (Name): Separately, there's 2v2, 3v3, and 4v4
[2020-12-15 17:30] velizar_ (Name): Separately, you can choose to self-penalize and play any of those modes with a self-imposed restriction, such as "only build one of those 3 units", which creates an interesting challenge and the strategies become totally different. There are many possible restrictions.
[2020-12-15 17:31] velizar_ (Name): Separately there is Archon mode, where you and another player share the same base vs two other players in a "1v1" - since Starcraft 2 is so hard, even 2 players can't control perfectly
[2020-12-15 17:32] velizar_ (Name): (In some of those you have hero units which you can level up, but that doesn't really count because it's too easy; however it counts if you have a hero unit + a whole base & army to manage)
[2020-12-15 17:32] velizar_ (Name): For each of the things I listed, there is a number of possible strategies which can work, and the skill ceiling is basically unreachably high (pro gamers can beat top difficulty + self-imposed penalties).
[2020-12-15 17:33] velizar_ (Name): It's like variants of the same game, but they're mind-blowingly different, and yet the fundamentals of micro-management and base management are kind of the same.
[2020-12-15 17:34] velizar_ (Name): (there's also free for all, but it kinda sucks)
[2020-12-15 17:34] velizar_ (Name): (there's also arcade mode where you play custom maps, but I don't know whether they offer much strategical depth)
[2020-12-15 17:35] velizar_ (Name): It's like Prismata's events where you alter one thing - say, all units have +1 build time - and that completely changes the whole meta of the game and suddenly you have to completely learn everything again.

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: The most commonly played game mode of Starcraft 2 is 1v1, where you start with just a base and a few workers;

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: Starcraft 2 has campaign, which has a variety of things - sometimes you don't have a base and only need to micro-manage your army; sometimes you start with a base, and generally have a different army from 1vs1; you can even customize your units with spendable currency

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: and you can also get global-use abilities which are also customizable

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: in that you don't usually need to kill your opponent, instead you have some objective and sometimes you're on a timer

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: Separately, there's co-op mode where you and another human play, and you each have about 15 different "races" to choose from, and play a variety of possible scenarios - those "races" have very different play styles, and a variety of strategies, and they synergize differently with each other, also the enemy unit composition varies from game to game;

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: Separately, there's 2v2, 3v3, and 4v4

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: Separately, you can choose to self-penalize and play any of those modes with a self-imposed restriction, such as "only build one of those 3 units", which creates an interesting challenge and the strategies become totally different. There are many possible restrictions.

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: Separately there is Archon mode, where you and another player share the same base vs two other players in a "1v1" - since Starcraft 2 is so hard, even 2 players can't control perfectly

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: (In some of those you have hero units which you can level up, but that doesn't really count because it's too easy; however it counts if you have a hero unit + a whole base & army to manage)

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: For each of the things I listed, there is a number of possible strategies which can work, and the skill ceiling is basically unreachably high (pro gamers can beat top difficulty + self-imposed penalties).

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: It's like variants of the same game, but they're mind-blowingly different, and yet the fundamentals of micro-management and base management are kind of the same.

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: (there's also free for all, but it kinda sucks)

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: (there's also arcade mode where you play custom maps, but I don't know whether they offer much strategical depth)

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: It's like Prismata's events where you alter one thing - say, all units have +1 build time - and that completely changes the whole meta of the game and suddenly you have to completely learn everything again.

--- Thread (1 messages, 2020-12-31) ---
[2020-12-31 00:05] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): it's the cheapest blue sink. low econ sets is where I think it can really shine. 

the ability to "switch out" of blue for 1 or a few turns if needed (this is similar to the sink argument but I see a difference since it's at a different scale, but if you want to lump them together then this is an expansion of the sink argument). 

I know Pixie wouldn't be eliminated so this argument applies to any unit that can create Pixies, but the ability to "exploit" some defenses is of value. 

to me, that makes it an interesting unit
